questions.q: The Rocky Linux courseware is published under which licence?
options: Creative Commons BY-SA
GPLv3
MIT
Proprietary - all rights reserved
questions.why: CC BY-SA: free to share and transform the material, while respecting the author's rights.

questions.q: In CC BY-SA, what does the BY (Attribution) part require of you?
options: You must cite the name of the original author
You must buy a licence before redistributing
You may only use the material for non-commercial purposes
You must submit your changes back upstream
questions.why: BY = Attribution: cite the original author. SA = Share Alike.

questions.q: Why does the guide give the vi editor a whole chapter of its own?
options: Other commands sometimes use identical syntax to vi (sed comes to mind)
vi is the only text editor available on Rocky Linux
vi is required to compile the kernel
Graphical editors cannot edit configuration files
questions.why: Knowing vi's essentials also pays off elsewhere - e.g. sed shares its syntax. nano is the simpler alternative, installed by default.

questions.q: Which two backup tools does the guide investigate in its backup chapter?
options: tar and cpio
rsnapshot and lsyncd
dd and dump
borg and restic
questions.why: tar, and the less widespread cpio. (rsnapshot/lsyncd are named as enhancement software, not the tools investigated.)

questions.q: Why does the guide call System Startup an important read?
options: Boot-time management has evolved significantly since the arrival of systemd
Rocky Linux boots differently from every other distribution
The BIOS must be reflashed on every kernel update
Startup scripts must be rewritten after every reboot
questions.why: Management of the system during the boot process changed a lot with systemd.
